name: dependency_manifest_and_execution
outcome_goal: Give dependency manifest truth and execution-runtime behavior one explicit contract so setup, packaging, and execution read the same declared dependency authority and prove the backend-agnostic seam.
anti_requirements: Do not treat the UI app manifest generator as dependency authority.
Do not keep setup-time manifest guesses separate from runtime dependency truth.
Do not leak backend-specific details through the execution seam.

jobs.label: dependency_manifest_truth_contract
jobs.outcome_goal: Make setup, packaging, and runtime all read the same dependency manifest truth.
jobs.anti_vibe_rule: One dependency authority only; no second manifest layer and no runtime guesswork.
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE:
Collapse dependency truth into one manifest and one startup/reporting surface.

TASK:
Review and adjust the dependency manifest contract so `pyproject.toml`, `requirements.runtime.txt`, `runtime/dependency_contract.py`, `runtime/embedding_service.py`, and `surfaces/_subsystems_base.py` all describe the same authoritative dependency state. Make sure orient/doctor and subsystem startup read that same truth, not a second derived list.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Do not reintroduce a second dependency manifest.
- Do not treat startup syncs as authority.
- Do not widen into unrelated app-manifest generation.

OUTPUT_CONTRACT:
Updated dependency truth code paths and tests that prove the manifest, startup wiring, and dependency-report surfaces all point at the same authority.

ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA:
- `dependency_truth_report()` and subsystem startup read the same manifest authority.
- `orient` and `doctor` surface dependency truth explicitly.
- The embedding authority stays explicit and testable.
- Targeted pytest on dependency truth and startup wiring passes.

STOP_BOUNDARY:
Do not rewrite UI manifest generation or add a parallel dependency source of truth.

jobs.label: dependency_execution_runtime_seam
jobs.outcome_goal: Keep execution runtime behind one backend-agnostic seam so Docker, CLI, and agent routing stay explicit instead of leaking backend details.
jobs.anti_vibe_rule: No backend folklore; keep the transport contract observable and explicit.
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE:
Keep execution runtime behind one backend-agnostic seam.

TASK:
Concentrate transport, timeout, kill, and agent-selection behavior behind `docker_runner.py`, `cli_llm.py`, `registry/agent_config.py`, and `runtime/execution_transport.py` so the runtime exposes generic execution labels instead of backend folklore.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Do not replace Docker or the CLI backend in this packet.
- Do not leak provider-specific defaults through the execution contract.
- Do not widen into the dependency manifest work above.

OUTPUT_CONTRACT:
Runtime seam changes and tests that prove execution transport is backend-agnostic and still observable.

ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA:
- Transport labels stay backend-agnostic.
- Docker host fallback remains explicit.
- CLI and agent routing still resolve through one contract.
- Targeted pytest on docker runner, transport parity, and agent config passes.

STOP_BOUNDARY:
Do not turn this into a backend swap or a provider-catalog cleanup.
